news.title: Tesla sales rose last quarter in a possible sign the worst of the Musk backlash is behind it
news.summary: Tesla car sales rose over the past three months for the second straight quarter in a sign the worst of the damage from boycotts and rivals stealing market share could be behind it.  The electric vehicle maker run by Elon Musk reported Thursday that it delivered 480,126 cars, much higher than the 384,122 from a year ago, as Europeans furious at Musk's embrace of far-right political candidates began buying again.  Tesla's stock dipped slightly in early morning trading on Thursday.

news.title: Is the June jobs report 'disappointing' or 'fantastic' for the Fed?
news.summary: The US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported an addition of 57,000 non-farm payroll jobs in June, well below economists' forecasts of 113,000. This report also included revisions on May's labor data, correcting May job gains of 172,000 down to 129,000. BMO Capital Markets senior economist Jennifer Lee, Morgan Stanley senior portfolio manager Vishal Khanduja, and Annex Wealth Management chief economist Brian Jacobsen come on The Morning Brief to discuss how the Federal Reserve may be interpreting this key set of economic data.
